body_md:
## Team & history

### Founder
**Mara Voss** is Ferrite's sole founder: technical, based in Berlin [C1]. She is a first-time founder with no prior fundraise [C2]. Background is self-reported only: four years of infrastructure work at a mid-size fintech, including an internal remote-cache that she says cut CI spend ~60% [C3]. That employment and impact claim is **unverified** on file—no manager/peer reference or employment artifact is attached.

### Execution residue (independent)
What the record can verify about her as an operator is shipping and public presence, not pedigree:

- Public repo history shows sustained, founder-dominated delivery: **1,400 commits over 11 months**, **86% by the founder**, **weekly releases for the last 20 weeks**, plus CI with real test coverage [C4]. The same archive shows **9 external contributors**, **412 stars**, **61 forks**—contributors, not a disclosed exec team [C5].
- May 2026: **Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon** infrastructure-track winner (~400 teams) for Ferrite as content-addressed caching for agent CI [C6].
- 2 June 2026 launch: aggregator front page ~6 hours; founder answered **41** in-thread comments with technical depth; **three** commenters self-identified as production users [C7].

An illustrative Founder Score fixture on file marks `cold_start: true` and scores **71 [58, 80]** (medium confidence), explicitly tying the score to shipping cadence and the hackathon win—not to deck traction [C8]. That fixture is labeled fictional / not Memory-layer output; weigh, do not treat as an independent underwriting authority.

### Exec team & single-founder red flag
No co-founder, CTO/CEO split, or other named executives appear in the application or archived signals [C1]. The operating footprint is Mara plus open-source contributors on the public repo [C5]. Example Fund I's stated posture puts pre-track-record / cold-start technical founders in scope and prioritizes shipping velocity over pedigree; solo founding is therefore a diligence concentration risk, not an automatic disqualifier under that thesis.

Independently archived residue available for solo-founder assessment: weekly shipping, the hackathon win, and launch-thread self-identified production users [C9]. Deck installs/MRR are not part of that independent residue. Agent-derived forum adjudication (deliberation input, not a company signal) left a **stable fork**: write $100k on that residue vs wait for reconciled billing/telemetry. Comfort with the single-founder concentration is therefore **conditional** on the same-week user-contact + billing/telemetry instrument collapsing that fork—not settled by pedigree or by the fixture score.

### Company timeline (founding → today)
Exact legal incorporation / founding date is **not disclosed**. Observable product history as of mid-July 2026:

| When | What | Trust |
|------|------|--------|
| ~11 months of commit history (through mid-July 2026 observation) | Founder-dominated build of the public Ferrite repo | verified-online [C4] |
| May 2026 | Hack-Nation infrastructure-track win | verified-online [C6] |
| 2 June 2026 | Public launch; self-identified production users in-thread | verified-online [C7] |
| 17 July 2026 | Applied for $500k pre-seed / $100k fast check (solo technical founder, no prior fundraise) | subject application [C1][C2] |

No prior institutional round is claimed; none is evidenced on file beyond the cold-start flag [C2][C8].

claims.text: Mara Voss is Ferrite's solo technical founder, located in Berlin, DE.
Mara Voss is a first-time founder with no prior fundraise.

evidence.quote: cold_start true (no prior fundraise, no prior company)

claims.text: Founder claims four years of infra at a mid-size fintech and authorship of an internal remote-cache that cut CI spend 60%.

evidence.quote: Ex-infra at a mid-size fintech (4 years), wrote the internal remote-cache that cut CI spend 60%.

claims.text: Public Ferrite repository shows 1,400 commits over 11 months with 86% authored by the founder, and weekly release cadence for the last 20 weeks, with CI config showing real test coverage.

evidence.quote: 1,400 commits over 11 months, 86% by the founder. Weekly release cadence for the last 20 weeks. CI config shows real test coverage.

claims.text: Public Ferrite repository has 412 stars, 61 forks, and 9 external contributors.
Ferrite won the infrastructure track at Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon May 2026 in a judged field of ~400 teams.

evidence.quote: Winner, infrastructure track, Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon May 2026 — 'Ferrite: content-addressed caching for agent CI'. Judged field of ~400 teams.

claims.text: On 2 June 2026 launch, the founder answered 41 comments in-thread with technical depth; three commenters identified themselves as production users.

evidence.quote: Launch post, 2nd June: front page of a major aggregator for ~6 hours, 214 points, 89 comments. Founder answered 41 comments in-thread with technical depth; three commenters identified themselves as production users.

claims.text: Illustrative Founder Score fixture lists score 71 [58, 80], confidence medium, cold_start true, with rationale pointing at shipping cadence and hackathon win; fixture is explicitly not Memory-layer output.

evidence.quote: FICTIONAL FIXTURE — illustrative Founder Score for the invented Ferrite deal: 71 [58, 80], confidence medium, cold_start true (no prior fundraise, no prior company), rationale: shipping cadence (sig-002) and hackathon win (sig-003). Hand-written example of the founder.schema shape; NOT produced by the Memory layer (real Memory records live in memory/founders.json).

claims.text: Independently archived signals show weekly release cadence for the last 20 weeks, a May 2026 Hack-Nation infrastructure-track win, and three launch-thread commenters who identified themselves as production users.

evidence.quote: Weekly release cadence for the last 20 weeks.
Winner, infrastructure track, Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon May 2026 — 'Ferrite: content-addressed caching for agent CI'. Judged field of ~400 teams.
three commenters identified themselves as production users.

gaps.note: Not disclosed; timeline reconstructed only from repo commit span, hackathon, launch, and application date.
Application states solo founder; no other named executives, advisors with equity, or org chart on file.
Fintech infra tenure and ~60% CI-spend claim are subject-only; no employment verification, manager/peer reference, or internal artifact attached (triage routed check open).

gaps.field: Prior company history beyond cold-start flag
gaps.note: No prior company names, exits, or failed ventures on file beyond 'no prior company' in the illustrative Founder Score fixture.
Three self-identified production users noted in archived launch thread; no outbound contact notes or customer references on file yet (instrument for the residual fork).

Stable-fork-plus-instrument outcome (write on shipping residue vs wait for billing/telemetry) is agent-derived deliberation input, not an archived independent signal; solo-founder underwriting comfort remains conditional on that instrument.
